goals.g1: Decide whether the reasoning_engine internal world-model (typed concept graph + role-uses) should be transplanted into ERS as a semantic-consistency check layer, given ERS currently checks reasoning SHAPE only.

prep_note: Archived flood-and-stall engine had no correctness oracle; ERS shape-check cannot catch a well-formed but false claim. Literature: grounding at the population boundary is the paradigm's unsolved failure mode.

prior_candidates.statement: Loop variants (probabilistic firing, spontaneous association) are studied between clean benchmark runs by editing the fixed condition, never via an in-engine swappable parameter, and any weights used must be fixed/mechanical, never judged from state.

derived_from.rule: a runtime parameter needs a chooser (f_regress); moving variant-selection to between-runs keeps the chooser outside the engine (the human experimenter), breaking the regress

goals: Fix the generator loop's firing discipline so it produces questions without spawning a regress of decision-makers.

prior_candidates.statement: 'Fire contingency on convergence' requires a detector that judges whether the state is converging.
goals: Fix the generator loop's firing discipline so it produces questions without spawning a regress of decision-makers.

prior_candidates.statement: Every drive fires on a hard read off existing machinery; no firing condition may require a judgment. Contingency fires on a fixed counter threshold; all other drives fire unconditionally; the counter alone contains volume.

derived_from.rule: a judged firing condition reintroduces the regress (f_regress); a counter-threshold fire does not (f_counter_hard); therefore replace the judged convergence gate with a hard threshold

goals: Fix the generator loop's firing discipline so it produces questions without spawning a regress of decision-makers.

prior_candidates.statement: Any adjustable knob requires a decision-maker to set it, and that decision-maker itself requires governing; if the governing is judgment it never bottoms out (regress).
goals: Fix the generator loop's firing discipline so it produces questions without spawning a regress of decision-makers.

prior_candidates.statement: The per-tree counter is an existing mechanical count read off state with no evaluation.
goals: Fix the generator loop's firing discipline so it produces questions without spawning a regress of decision-makers.

f_ers_shape.statement: ERS today checks reasoning SHAPE (grounded, has-falsifier, no-orphan) and does not model what the reasoner asserts to be true about the world.

f_shape_gap.statement: A claim can be shape-valid and semantically false simultaneously; shape-checking cannot distinguish them.

f_wm_exists.statement: reasoning_engine already contains a typed world-model: concepts, role-uses on edges, and typed relations, sufficient to test whether a referent exists and whether an asserted relation contradicts a populated one.

f_grounding_unsolved.statement: Independent literature reports grounding failure (referents not matching context, hallucinated propositions) as the dominant failure mode even when the LLM is confined to pure translation.

ans.statement: Transplant the world-model as a CONSISTENCY layer beside ERS: a claim is checked for whether its referents exist in the asserted model and whether it contradicts an already-populated relation. It checks consistency-against-asserted-model, NOT truth-against-world. Population of the model stays the human/reasoner's job, not ERS's.

derived_from.rule: shape-check has a known gap (f_shape_gap); the world-model can test the missing dimension (f_wm_exists); add it as a distinct consistency layer without claiming it closes the truth gap

cand_ans.statement: Add world-model as an ERS consistency-check layer (not a truth oracle).
cand_ans.discriminator: vs. leaving ERS shape-only, or vs. claiming full semantic verification

chk_falsify.method: Find a case where the consistency layer gives false assurance — passes a claim that is still false.
chk_falsify.result: A claim can be fully consistent with the asserted model AND false, if the model itself is wrong (garbage-in). So the layer does NOT catch truth errors; it only catches claims inconsistent with what was asserted. This is a real limit, not a defect of the proposal — it is exactly why o_no_oracle_overclaim exists. The proposal survives ONLY in its narrowed form (consistency, not truth); the broad form (verifies correctness) is falsified.

side_findings.finding: broad 'verifies truth' reading is false; only the narrow consistency claim holds

chk_oblige.method: Does it satisfy o_adds_power / o_no_oracle_overclaim / o_bounded?
chk_oblige.result: o_adds_power: yes — catches referent-nonexistence and relation-contradiction, neither reachable by shape-check. o_no_oracle_overclaim: satisfied by construction (consistency-only). o_bounded: satisfied — ERS reads an already-populated model, it does not populate it.

chk_relevance.method: Tie the answer to the goal.
chk_relevance.result: Goal asked whether to transplant the world-model into ERS. Answer says yes but only as a consistency layer with an explicit scope boundary — directly discharging the decision with its own limit named.

obligations.o_adds_power: must let ERS catch at least one error class it provably cannot catch now
obligations.o_no_oracle_overclaim: must not claim to verify TRUTH against the world — only consistency against the reasoner's own asserted model
obligations.o_bounded: must not require ERS to itself populate the world-model via LLM (that reintroduces the unsolved grounding problem inside the checker)

committed.statement: Transplant the world-model as a CONSISTENCY layer beside ERS: a claim is checked for whether its referents exist in the asserted model and whether it contradicts an already-populated relation. It checks consistency-against-asserted-model, NOT truth-against-world. Population of the model stays the human/reasoner's job, not ERS's.
